1718-AENTR/1719-AENTR - Denial of Service
Severity:
High
Advisory ID:
SD1778
Published Date:
July 14, 2026
Last Updated:
July 14, 2026
Revision Number:
1.0

No

Yes

Downloads
The following link(s) provide the security advisory in
Vulnerability Exploitability Exchange
format:
JSON
Summary
The security of our products is important to us as your industrial automation supplier. This security issue was found internally during routine testing and is being reported based on our commitment to customer transparency and improvement of all business environments.
Product Description
The 1719-AENTR from Rockwell Automation is an EtherNet/IP adapter for the 1719 Ex I/O system, enabling distributed I/O communication over EtherNet/IP in hazardous location environments. It serves as the network interface between 1719 Ex I/O modules and the control system.
Affected Products and Solution
Affected Product
CVE
Affected Firmware Version
Corrected in Software Version
Affected Catalog Numbers
1718/ 1719 Ex I/O EtherNet/IP Adapter
CVE-2026-9140
3.011
3.012
1718-AENTR/ 1719-AENTR
Security Issue Details for CVE-2026-9140
Category
Details
CVE ID
CVE-2026-9140
Impact
A denial-of-service security issue exists in the 1719-AENTR. The security issue stems from improper handling of a UDP unicast network storm, which causes the device to become overloaded and lose communication. A power cycle is required to recover.
CVSS 3.1 Base Score
7.5/10
CVSS 4.0 Base Score
8.7/10
CWE
CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
Known Exploited Vulnerability
No
Mitigations and Workarounds
Customers using the affected software, who are not able to upgrade to one of the corrected versions, should use our
security best practices
.

Denial-of-Service (DoS):
An attack that disrupts the normal functioning of a system, often by overwhelming it with requests.
